# {{< i18n wbs_dictionary >}} {}

## Purpose of This Document

This WBS Dictionary provides a **detailed description of each WBS work
package**, defining:

- Scope and boundaries
- Key activities
- Deliverables
- Acceptance criteria
- Responsible roles
- Explicit exclusions

This document, together with the **WBS**, constitutes the **Scope Baseline** of
the project and must be approved **before finalization of the Project Management
Plan (PMP)**.

---

## WBS Dictionary Structure

Each entry includes:

- WBS ID
- Work Package Name
- Description
- Key Activities
- Deliverables
- Acceptance Criteria
- Responsible Role(s)
- Explicit Exclusions

---

## WBS Dictionary Entries

### WBS ID: 1.1  

**Work Package:** Initiation & Strategy  

**Description**  

Defines the strategic, environmental, and governance foundation of the project.

**Key Activities**

- Develop and approve Project Charter
- Develop Business Case
- Define Strategy & Governance (Value Stream, OKRs)
- Identify and register stakeholders

**Deliverables**

- Approved Project Charter
- Approved Business Case
- Strategy & Governance documents
- Stakeholder Register

**Acceptance Criteria**

- Formal sponsor approval
- Alignment with SUB environmental strategy

**Responsible Roles**

- Project Sponsor (NOAA / SUB)
- Project Manager

**Explicit Exclusions**

- Technical solution design
- Model development

---

### WBS ID: 1.2  

**Work Package:** Governance & Value Management  

**Description**  

Establishes value measurement, KPI baselines, and decision governance.

**Key Activities**

- Define OKRs and KRs
- Define executive KPIs
- Establish KPI baselines and measurement methods
- Define Stage‑Gate framework

**Deliverables**

- OKR documentation
- KPI framework
- Stage‑Gate governance model

**Acceptance Criteria**

- KPIs measurable and auditable
- Ecological KPIs prioritized

**Responsible Roles**

- Product Owner
- Project Sponsor
- Environmental SME Lead

**Explicit Exclusions**

- Day‑to‑day project execution
- Technical metric dashboards

---

### WBS ID: 1.3  

**Work Package:** Data & Environmental Preparation  

**Description**  

Prepares subsea data and environmental context for safe AI usage.

**Key Activities**

- AUV image ingestion
- Metadata and spatial data preparation
- Image quality assessment
- Definition of environmental annotation guidelines

**Deliverables**

- Ingested and cataloged datasets
- Quality assessment reports
- Environmental annotation guidelines

**Acceptance Criteria**

- Data completeness verified
- SME approval of annotation guidelines

**Responsible Roles**

- Data Engineering Lead
- Environmental SME Lead

**Explicit Exclusions**

- Model training
- Automated decision-making

---

### WBS ID: 1.4  

**Work Package:** AI Model Development  

**Description**  

Develops the AI/CV capability for automated screening, under ecological
constraints.

**Key Activities**

- AI-assisted annotation (HITL)
- Model training (binary classification)
- Precision-oriented inference pipeline
- Explainability mechanisms

**Deliverables**

- Trained screening model
- Inference pipeline
- Model documentation

**Acceptance Criteria**

- Meets minimum performance criteria
- False-negative risk controlled

**Responsible Roles**

- Data Science Lead
- Environmental SME Lead (validation)

**Explicit Exclusions**

- Full taxonomic classification
- Autonomous decisions without validation

---

### WBS ID: 1.5  

**Work Package:** Validation & Ecological Assurance  

**Description**  

Ensures ecological validity and risk control of automated screening.

**Key Activities**

- Model performance evaluation
- Ecological validation by SMEs
- Error analysis and mitigation
- MVP ecological acceptance

**Deliverables**

- Validation reports
- Ecological acceptance decision
- Risk mitigation actions

**Acceptance Criteria**

- KPI‑3 (Ecological Data Protection Index) ≥ threshold
- SME sign‑off

**Responsible Roles**

- Environmental SME Lead
- Project Sponsor (acceptance)

**Explicit Exclusions**

- Scaling decisions
- Operational deployment

---

### WBS ID: 1.6  

**Work Package:** Digital Asset Consolidation  

**Description**  

Transforms project outputs into reusable corporate environmental assets.

**Key Activities**

- Dataset versioning
- Model artifact management
- Traceability and audit support
- Reuse readiness assessment

**Deliverables**

- Versioned datasets
- Versioned model artifacts
- Traceability documentation

**Acceptance Criteria**

- ≥ 90% assets versioned and auditable
- Governance approval for reuse

**Responsible Roles**

- Data Engineering Lead
- SUB Data Governance

**Explicit Exclusions**

- Enterprise-wide rollout
- External data sharing

---

### WBS ID: 1.7  

**Work Package:** Project Management & Control  

**Description**  

Provides planning, monitoring, control, and closure of the project.

**Key Activities**

- Planning and monitoring
- Risk and issue management
- Communication and reporting
- Project closure and lessons learned

**Deliverables**

- Status reports
- Risk register updates
- Closure report

**Acceptance Criteria**

- PMBOK compliance
- Sponsor approval at closure

**Responsible Roles**

- Project Manager

**Explicit Exclusions**

- Technical execution
- Environmental validation

---

## WBS Dictionary Governance Rules

- Each work package has **one accountable owner**
- Changes require formal change control
- No work package may proceed past validation gates without ecological approval

---

## Scope Baseline Confirmation

Together, the following documents constitute the **Scope Baseline**:

- Project Scope Statement
- Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)
- WBS Dictionary
